prompt: List the current workspace root and summarize the important directories and files for a coding agent.

context.workspace_rule: Use only relative paths in the MCP workspace

plan.goal: Enumerate the workspace root and produce a coding-agent-oriented summary that buckets every top-level entry by role (entry points, config, source, docs, tests, scripts, tooling/ignore) with concrete evidence.
exploration_checks: Call list_directory('.') to capture every top-level entry with [DIR]/[FILE] markers
Read project metadata to confirm stack and purpose: read_file on README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION
Read likely entry points (head only, ~60 lines): start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh
List each non-hidden source subdirectory once: list_directory on app/, worker/, unified/, chatbai/, skillboss/, therouter/, wavespeed/, backup_gumloop_cli/, docs/
Resolve mystery root files with file_info on 'ns' and 'unifiedme' (no extension)
Note .git/, .venv/, .sisyphus/, .mcp_server.log as tooling/ignore without descending into them
execution_steps: Run list_directory('.') and record all entries
For each metadata file (README.md, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, VERSION), call read_file and extract project name, language version, key dependencies, declared purpose
For each entry-point candidate (start.py, mcp_server.py, login.py, start.sh), call read_file with limit~60 to capture module docstring / shebang / first imports
For each top-level source directory, call list_directory and capture up to ~20 entries to infer its role
Call file_info on 'ns' and 'unifiedme' to determine if they are scripts, binaries, or stray files

Group every root entry into one of these buckets with one-line evidence: entry-points, config/deploy, source modules, instrumentation/interception, tests, utility scripts, documentation, runtime artifacts, environment/tooling (ignore)

Compose the final summary: project type & stack (from README/requirements), main entry points (with file paths), source-directory map (with inferred roles + observed contents), docs cluster, test cluster, ignore list — explicitly flag any item still not directly inspected
validation_checks: Every one of the 66 top-level entries appears in exactly one bucket in the final summary
Each bucket label is backed by either file contents read or directory listing observed (no claims from filename alone unless explicitly flagged as inferred)
Project stack (language, framework, deploy target) is stated based on README.md and requirements.txt contents, not just filenames
'ns' and 'unifiedme' are resolved via file_info (size + type) or flagged as still unknown
Subdirectories that were not listed are explicitly named as un-inspected — no fabricated role descriptions
Summary distinguishes confirmed facts (read/listed) from inferred facts (named-only) with clear language

raw: {"status":"needs_tool","tool":"list_directory","arguments":{"path":"."},"reason":"Start exploration by enumerating every top-level entry in the workspace root before bucketing them."}

raw: {"status":"needs_tool","tool":"read_file","arguments":{"path":"README.md","limit":80},"reason":"Root listing captured. Next exploration check is to read README.md to confirm project name, stack, and stated purpose before bucketing entries."}

action.reason: Root listing captured. Next exploration check is to read README.md to confirm project name, stack, and stated purpose before bucketing entries.

result.text:
1: # Unified AI Proxy
2: 
3: Single OpenAI-compatible endpoint that routes to multiple AI providers with account rotation, credit tracking, and centralized license management.
4: 
5: ## Providers
6: 
7: | Tier | Provider | Models |
8: |------|----------|--------|
9: | Standard | Kiro (AWS Q) | `claude-sonnet-4`, `deepseek-3.2`, `qwen3-coder-next` |
10: | Max | CodeBuddy | `claude-opus-4.6`, `gpt-5.4`, `gemini-2.5-pro` |
11: | WaveSpeed | WaveSpeed LLM | `new-claude-opus-4.7`, `new-gpt-5.2` |
12: | Max GL | Gumloop | `gl-claude-opus-4-7`, `gl-gpt-5.4` |
13: 
14: ## Quick Install
15: 
16: ```bash
17: curl -sSL https://unified-api.roubot71.workers.dev/install | bash
18: ```
19: 
20: Requirements: Python 3.10+, Git, curl.
21: 
22: ## Commands
23: 
24: ```bash
25: unifiedme run          # Start proxy (foreground, interactive)
26: unifiedme start        # Start proxy (background daemon)
27: unifiedme stop         # Stop background proxy
28: unifiedme status       # Show status, version, uptime
29: unifiedme update       # Pull latest code + reinstall deps
30: unifiedme kill-port    # Kill zombie process on port 1430
31: unifiedme logout       # Clear license key (switch license)
32: unifiedme help         # Show help
33: ```
34: 
35: ## First Time Setup
36: 
37: ```
38: 1. unifiedme run
39: 2. Enter your license key (UNIF-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX)
40: 3. Open http://localhost:1430/dashboard
41: 4. Set your admin password
42: 5. Copy your API key (sk-xxx)
43: 6. Use in your IDE/CLI:
44:    Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
45: ```
46: 
47: ## API Endpoints
48: 
49: ```
50: POST /v1/chat/completions    OpenAI-compatible chat (routes by model)
51: POST /v1/messages            Anthropic-compatible messages
52: GET  /v1/models              List available models
53: GET  /dashboard              Admin dashboard
54: ```
55: 
56: ## Architecture
57: 
58: ```
59: Client (IDE/CLI)
60:   |
61:   POST /v1/chat/completions
62:   Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
63:   |
64: Unified Proxy (:1430)
65:   |-- Model Router (by model name -> tier)
66:   |-- Account Manager (sticky rotation, auto-ban)
67:   |-- Message Filter (find/replace before upstream)
68:   |-- Watchword Scanner (alert on keywords)
69:   |
70:   |-- Kiro Proxy     -> AWS Q API (direct Python)
71:   |-- CodeBuddy Proxy -> codebuddy.ai
72:   |-- WaveSpeed Proxy -> wavespeed.ai
73:   |-- Gumloop Proxy   -> WebSocket + Firebase auth
74:   |
75:   |-- License Client  -> Central D1 Database
76:       (sync accounts, usage logs, alerts every 5 min)
77: ```
78: 
79: ## License System
80:

raw: I'm getting a clearer picture of the project structure now—it's a unified proxy that routes requests to multiple AI providers through a single OpenAI-compatible interface, with `unifiedme` as the CLI tool, a Cloudflare Worker handling the backend routing, and separate modules for each provider like WaveSpeed.{"status":"needs_tool","tool":"read_file","arguments":{"path":"requirements.txt"},"reason":"README confirms project is 'Unified AI Proxy' (Python 3.10+, OpenAI-compatible router). Reading requirements.txt to confirm framework choice (FastAPI vs Flask vs others) and key dependencies before bucketing."}

action.reason: README confirms project is 'Unified AI Proxy' (Python 3.10+, OpenAI-compatible router). Reading requirements.txt to confirm framework choice (FastAPI vs Flask vs others) and key dependencies before bucketing.

14:

raw:
I'm mapping out the full tech stack now—FastAPI with uvicorn for the async web server, httpx and aiohttp for async HTTP calls, aiosqlite for the database layer, pydantic for validation and config management, websockets and 2captcha for the Gumloop chat integration with captcha solving, FastMCP as the MCP server framework, asyncssh for SSH/VPS operations, and camoufox with playwright and browserforge for automating batch account logins across browsers.

Now I need to examine the app/ directory structure to understand how the FastAPI application is organized.{"status":"needs_tool","tool":"list_directory","arguments":{"path":"app"},"reason":"requirements.txt confirms FastAPI + uvicorn + aiosqlite stack with FastMCP, websockets+2captcha (Gumloop), asyncssh (VPS), and camoufox/playwright (browser automation). 'app/' is the most likely location of the FastAPI proxy code — listing it to verify before bucketing source directories."}

action.reason: requirements.txt confirms FastAPI + uvicorn + aiosqlite stack with FastMCP, websockets+2captcha (Gumloop), asyncssh (VPS), and camoufox/playwright (browser automation). 'app/' is the most likely location of the FastAPI proxy code — listing it to verify before bucketing source directories.

raw: I'm seeing that app/ is a Python package with provider adapters across several subdirectories. I need to explore the remaining directories like worker/ and unified/, but the tool constraints mean I can only make one call at a time, so I'm prioritizing which ones to check first.{"status":"needs_tool","tool":"list_directory","arguments":{"path":"unified"},"reason":"app/ contains browser.py + providers/ + errors/ (FastAPI provider adapters). 'unified/' shares the project name and is the next-most-likely main package — listing it to determine whether it or app/ holds the FastAPI entry point."}

action.reason: app/ contains browser.py + providers/ + errors/ (FastAPI provider adapters). 'unified/' shares the project name and is the next-most-likely main package — listing it to determine whether it or app/ holds the FastAPI entry point.
